key_insight: The paper predicts future weld-pool regions 500 ms ahead by combining physics-guided state-space temporal modeling with process and sensor signals for real-time laser wire-feed welding segmentation.
engineering_relevance: This enables more reliable closed-loop welding control by compensating for sensing and actuation delay with lookahead perception of keyhole, wire, and molten-pool geometry.
method: Uses historical coaxial grayscale images plus welding parameters and aligned wire-state electrical signals as inputs.
Applies a spatiotemporal state-space network with process/sensor-conditioned normalization and patch-level temporal modeling.
Adds dense future feature prediction, motion-aware decoding, and auxiliary geometry/consistency losses to stabilize future masks.
evidence_signal: On a 43-sequence laser welding dataset, WeldMamba achieved 74.63% mIoU at a 500 ms lookahead, with ablations showing temporal history, patch-level state-space modeling, and keyhole motion awareness as key contributors.
watch_item: Performance depends on synchronized multimodal sensing and may be sensitive to domain shift or timing misalignment in real welding setups.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Spatiotemporal State Space Modeling for Lookahead Molten Pool Segmentation in Laser Wire-Feed Welding
